# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent elasticsearch exposed apis.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# ============================================================
# Elasticsearch Production Configuration
# ============================================================

# ---- Cluster Settings ----
cluster.name: production-search-cluster
node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
node.roles: [master, data, ingest]

# ---- Path Settings ----
path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch/data
path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch
path.repo: ["/mnt/backups/elasticsearch"]

# ---- Network Settings ----
# Bind to specific internal network interface rather than all interfaces
# Using site-local address to prevent exposure to public internet
network.host: _site_
http.port: 9200
transport.port: 9300

# ---- CORS Settings ----
# Enable CORS with explicit allowlist of trusted origins only
http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "https://app.example.com,https://dashboard.example.com"
http.cors.allow-methods: "OPTIONS,HEAD,GET,POST,PUT,DELETE"
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With,Content-Type,Content-Length,Authorization"
http.cors.allow-credentials: true
http.cors.max-age: 1728000

# ---- Discovery & Cluster Formation ----
# Explicit list of master-eligible nodes for discovery (no multicast)
discovery.seed_hosts:
  - "es-node-01.internal.example.com:9300"
  - "es-node-02.internal.example.com:9300"
  - "es-node-03.internal.example.com:9300"

cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - "es-node-01"
  - "es-node-02"
  - "es-node-03"

# Minimum master nodes to prevent split-brain (majority of master-eligible nodes)
# In Elasticsearch 7.x+ this is handled automatically, but we set fault detection
cluster.fault_detection.leader_check.interval: 2s
cluster.fault_detection.leader_check.timeout: 10s
cluster.fault_detection.follower_check.interval: 2s
cluster.fault_detection.follower_check.timeout: 10s

# ---- Security Settings ----
# Enable Elasticsearch security features (authentication & authorization)
xpack.security.enabled: true

# Enable audit logging for security events
xpack.security.audit.enabled: true
xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.include:
  - "access_denied"
  - "access_granted"
  - "anonymous_access_denied"
  - "authentication_failed"
  - "connection_denied"
  - "tampered_request"
  - "run_as_denied"
  - "run_as_granted"
  - "security_config_change"

# TLS/SSL for HTTP layer (client-to-node encryption)
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.http.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/http-keystore.p12
xpack.security.http.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/http-truststore.p12
# Keystore passwords stored in Elasticsearch keystore (not plaintext)
# Use: elasticsearch-keystore add xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.secure_password
# Use: elasticsearch-keystore add xpack.security.http.ssl.truststore.secure_password

# TLS/SSL for Transport layer (node-to-node encryption)
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: full
xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/transport-keystore.p12
xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/certs/transport-truststore.p12
# Use: elasticsearch-keystore add xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.secure_password
# Use: elasticsearch-keystore add xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.secure_password

# ---- API Security ----
# Disable destructive operations requiring wildcard patterns
action.destructive_requires_name: true

# Disable dynamic scripting from untrusted sources
script.allowed_types: inline,stored
script.allowed_contexts: search,update,ingest

# ---- Memory & Performance ----
bootstrap.memory_lock: true

# ---- HTTP Security Headers ----
http.detailed_errors.enabled: false
http.max_content_length: 100mb
http.max_header_size: 8kb
http.max_initial_line_length: 4kb

# ---- Restrict remote cluster connections ----
# Only allow explicitly configured remote clusters
cluster.remote.connect: false

# ---- Index Settings ----
# Prevent automatic index creation to avoid accidental data exposure
action.auto_create_index: ".monitoring*,.watches,.triggered_watches,.watcher-history*,.ml*,.security*"

# ---- Logging (avoid logging sensitive data) ----
# Sensitive headers are not logged by default; ensure this remains the case
# Configure in log4j2.properties for detailed log management
